articles.title: HSBC to JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs: Why are foreign brokerages downgrading India?
articles.summary: Global brokerages are becoming cautious on India as geopolitical tensions and high crude oil prices impact the macro outlook. Nifty targets have been cut due to inflation and currency weakness, with JPMorgan and HSBC downgrading market ratings, citing risks to growth and demand.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Coal India share price jumps over 4% after Q4 results. Should you buy, sell or hold the PSU stock?
articles.summary: Coal India share price has gained over 5% in one month, and more than 17% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis. The PSU stock has jumped 20% in six months and has risen 18% in one year. Coal India share price has delivered multibagger returns of 101% in three years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices extend rise to 7th day; Brent near $110 as US-Iran peace talks stall. What's next?
articles.summary: MCX crude oil prices surged as much as 1% to ₹9,201 per barrel on Tuesday. Here's what experts said about the near-term outlook for crude oil prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold prices volatile on MCX as strong dollar, high oil prices weigh; US Fed policy outcome in focus
articles.summary: Gold price today: Gold rate traded volatily in the domestic futures market on Tuesday morning as elevated crude oil prices drove the US dollar higher, weighing on the yellow metal. Lingering uncertainty over the stalled US-Iran peace talks is keeping gold prices under pressure.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy for short term: From GMDC to Hindustan Zinc- Experts suggest 6 stock picks for next 1-2 weeks
articles.summary: Stocks to buy for short term: Experts highlight six stocks to consider for short-term investment. With solid fundamentals and favorable technical indicators, stocks like GMDC and Hindustan Zinc show promising potential for gains in the upcoming weeks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Silver rate today steady as dollar strengthens, oil surges ahead of US Fed meet: Target level ahead?
articles.summary: Silver rates remained steady on April 28 as investors awaited clarity on U.S.-Iran peace talks and central bank decisions. MCX silver fell 0.5% to ₹2,40,490 per kg, while gold prices were flat. Geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns influenced market dynamics.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Three consumer stocks where FIIs increased stake in March quarter
articles.summary: Rising FII ownership can indicate investor confidence and attract market participation. However, it should not be the sole consideration for equity investment. Investors should also assess valuations, earnings growth, debt, promoter quality, sector outlook, cash flow, and competitive strength.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints muted opening; BoJ, US Fed meet to gold, silver rates — eight stocks to buy or sell
articles.summary: Stock market today, 28 April 2026: Experts have recommended eight stocks to buy today — Aarti Industries, Aditya Infotech, Bajaj Finance, HDFC Bank, Jupiter Wagons, Thermax, TVS Motor Company, and Sarda Energy & Minerals
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Tata Group firm Trent announces record dates for bonus issue, dividend; check details
articles.summary: Trent Ltd., backed by Tata Group, set May 29, 2026, as the record date for its first bonus share issuance at a 1:2 ratio. Subject to approvals, shareholders will receive one bonus share for every two held. Trent also announced the record date for dividend payment.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Q4 results 2026: Maruti Suzuki to Eternal among companies to declare Q4 results today; full list here
articles.summary: Q4 results 2026: Around 40 companies will declare their Q4 results today. Here's a complete list of companies, along with previews.

articles.summary: Chandan Taparia has recommended three stocks to buy today, 28 April 2026 - JSW Energy, Sona BLW Precision Forgings and NMDC shares.

articles.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 28 April
articles.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a negative start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 24,046 level, a discount of nearly 74 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.

articles.title: Stocks to watch: Maruti Suzuki, Trent, Eternal, RailTel among 10 shares in focus today
articles.summary: Stock market today: Looking at the Gift Nifty trends, the Indian stock market is likely to snap its gaining streak on Tuesday, as the index signals a negative opening. Amid this backdrop, these stocks will remain in focus on Tuesday, 28 April.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: From Gift Nifty, crude oil prices, gold rates to dollar: 8 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
articles.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,051 level, a discount of nearly 69 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a negative start for the Indian stock market indices.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Korea Passes UK to Become World’s Eighth-Largest Stock Market
articles.summary: South Korea has leapfrogged the UK to become the world’s eighth-biggest stock market, fueled by a high-octane rally in its artificial intelligence-linked technology champions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: BoJ policy decision Highlights: Bank of Japan keeps interest rate unchanged at 0.75%; 3 board members call for rate hike
articles.summary: BoJ policy decision Highlights: The Bank of Japan kept the short-term interest rate target unchanged at 0.75%. However, three in the nine-member board proposed hiking borrowing costs, signalling the bank's concern over inflationary pressures from the US-Iran war in the Middle East.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today — 28 April 2026
articles.summary: The Indian stock market ended Monday's session on a positive note. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking recommends five breakout stocks to buy today.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The bond market is sleepy. A sharp move may be coming.
articles.summary: While testimony from Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh did nothing to wake up the sleepy Treasury market, there are catalysts for a breakout this week and beyond.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to trade: Raja Venkatraman recommends 3 stocks for 28 April
articles.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 28 April. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock recommendations for 28 April from MarketSmith India
articles.summary: MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 28 April. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: NBFCs may reflect monsoon stress before it shows up elsewhere
articles.summary: Dalal Street's reacts remains largely muted by poor monsoon forecasts as crude oil and capital flows dominate sentiment. But rural lenders tied to farm cash flows could reflect stress well before it shows up in consumption or earnings.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Restart of US-Iran Peace Talks
articles.summary: Gold fell as efforts to resume peace talks between the US and Iran remained at an impasse, two months into a war that’s upended global markets and raised inflation risks.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil advances with Middle East in focus, equities rise slightly
articles.summary: GLOBAL-MARKETS/ (WRAPUP 7):GLOBAL MARKETS-Oil advances with Middle East in focus, equities rise slightly
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: A Warsh-Led Fed Stands to Reignite the Treasuries Steepener Bet
articles.summary: Wall Street strategists say that a Kevin Warsh-led Federal Reserve may jolt the $31 trillion Treasuries market out of its narrow trading ranges, urging investors to position for shorter-dated yields to eventually move lower.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Canara Robeco AMC Q4 Results: Revenue rises 13%, PAT steady at ₹41 crore; declares ₹2.5 dividend
articles.summary: Canara Robeco Asset Management reported stable financial results for Q4FY26, with revenue of ₹114.20 crore and profit after tax of ₹41.36 crore. For FY26, revenues rose to ₹424.95 crore, with a profit of ₹203.80 crore. A final dividend of ₹2.50 was also announced.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Verizon shares rise over 3% after first quarter sales beat expectations
articles.summary: The carrier added 55,000 net new mobile phone users, marking its first positive first-quarter growth for this metric since 2013—a year-over-year jump of 340,000
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: COMEX gold, silver fall on rising crude, focus shifts to central bank meetings
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices fell on April 27 due to rising crude oil prices amid uncertainty over US-Iran peace talks, raising inflation fears. Major central banks are expected to maintain current interest rates, impacting gold's appeal as an inflation hedge.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Coal India Q4 Results: Net profit jumps 11% YoY to ₹10,839 crore; declares final dividend of ₹5.25 per share
articles.summary: Coal India reported a consolidated net profit of ₹10,839 crore for Q4, exceeding market expectations and marking an 11.15% increase from ₹9,751 crore year-on-year. The profit also rose 51.4% sequentially from ₹7,157 crore in the previous quarter.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50 hits 5% upper circuit after Q4 results 2024
articles.summary: AVI Polymers reached its 5% upper circuit limit, closing at ₹17.26 due to strong Q4 results, with revenue up 13.6% and net profit rising 46.1%. The company reported impressive FY26 growth, achieving a net profit of ₹20.33 crore and a 20.5-fold increase in net worth.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Wall Street edges lower as investors await big earnings amid stalled US-Iran talks
articles.summary: At 10:10 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.05%, the S&P 500 lost 0.07%, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.27%
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Adani Total Gas Q4 Results: Profit grows 9% YoY to ₹168 crore, revenue up 16.6%; margins contract
articles.summary: Adani Total Gas reported a Q4 net profit of ₹168.34 crore, up 9% year-on-year. Revenue rose 16.62% to ₹1,694.61 crore, while EBITDA increased to ₹300.32 crore. The EBITDA margin decreased to 17.72% from 18.30% last year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 futures flat as US-Iran peace talks stall; Brent climbs above $108
articles.summary: US stock futures dipped as ceasefire negotiations with Iran faltered. Despite record highs for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions are creating caution among investors. Earnings reports from major tech companies are anticipated this week.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Amba Auto Sales & Services IPO Day 1: Issue booked 11% so far; check GMP, price band & other details
articles.summary: Amba Auto Sales & Services IPO Day 1: The price band for Amba Auto Sales & Services IPO has been set at ₹130 to ₹135 per share. Retail investors need to apply for at least two lots of 1,000 shares each, resulting in a minimum payout of ₹135,000 at the upper end of the price band.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Axis Bank vs IndusInd vs IDFC First: Which bank stock should investors pick post Q4 results 2026?
articles.summary: Post Q4FY26, Axis Bank emerges as the top pick for conservative investors due to its strong performance and asset quality, while IndusInd Bank is seen as a high-risk turnaround option. IDFC First Bank shows stable metrics but faces earnings visibility challenges.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's fertilizer subsidy may jump 20% as Hormuz crisis spikes prices
articles.summary: India's fertilizer subsidy stood at ₹1.86 trillion in the 2025-26 fiscal year (FY26), and was estimated at ₹1.71 trillion in the budget for the ongoing financial year. Mint reported on 2 March that India's fertilizer subsidy will go up amid the West Asian conflict.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India seals FTA with New Zealand, gets $20 bn investment pledge, duty-free access
articles.summary: The agreement comes at a time of evolving global and regional dynamics, with New Zealand viewing India as a key partner in promoting regional stability, economic resilience and shared prosperity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India-New Zealand FTA: Temporary Employment Entry offers 5,000 work visas for skilled Indians — check key features
articles.summary: The TEE pathway caps the number of visas at 5,000 at any time, allowing stays of up to 3 years. It targets both traditional Indian expertise—such as yoga instructors, AYUSH practitioners, and chefs—and high-demand fields like IT, healthcare, engineering, and education.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mapping the fallout: Which Indian sectors face risks from the West Asia war?
articles.summary: The West Asia war has triggered an energy crisis, hitting sectors like fertilizers and airlines. With the Strait of Hormuz disrupted, rising costs and supply shortages could directly or indirectly impact nearly 30 sectors.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil jumps over 1% as US-Iran talks stall, Hormuz blockade deepens crisis
articles.summary: Crude prices rose over 1% after US-Iran talks stalled and Hormuz tensions deepened, raising fears of supply disruptions and sharper inventory draws.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India to overhaul firefighting infra, rejig its vehicles and equipment
articles.summary: The government is seeking to address critical gaps in fire safety infrastructure, amid acute shortages of fire stations, equipment and personnel, raising concerns over response readiness as urban fire risks rise.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The FTA cautionary tale: What India-Korea trade warns us about new global deals
articles.summary: In their bilateral trade pact, South Korea has gained significantly more than India. As it enters more such pacts, India would do well to review where it fell short with South Korea and why.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Kedaara picks up 5% in Axis Finance for Rs750 crore, sets benchmark for IPO-headed NBFC
articles.summary: Axis Finance has raised Rs750 crore from Kedaara Capital for a 5.08% stake, marking its first external funding. This capital boost strengthens its financial base as it jostles in India's extremely competitive credit market—preparing for a future IPO under regulatory requirements.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Fertilizer output, IT earnings, health spending survey
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre raises wheat procurement target to 34.5 million tonnes amid erratic weather
articles.summary: The government has raised procurement target even as unseasonal rains raise output concerns.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Oil prices up 1% amid continued stalemate on US-Iran talks, closure of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Crude prices climbed over 1% as supply disruption in the Strait of Hormuz and stalled US-Iran talks kept markets on edge, raising concerns over India’s import bill and fuel marketing losses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI sees growth, inflation risks from escalating US-Iran war
articles.summary: Further escalation, a longer duration and a wider spread of the war are key risks to the global outlook, says RBI in its April Bulletin
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Power demand spikes even before peak summer, seen at record high this year
articles.summary: India’s power demand has surged to nearly 240 GW amid an early, intense heatwave, nearing last year’s peak. With temperatures set to stay above normal, demand could hit record highs, testing grid capacity despite rising supply and preparedness by power utilities.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Indian private sector output growth accelerates in April as manufacturing grows faster: HSBC Flash India PMI
articles.summary: April saw a rebound in India's private sector growth. The HSBC Flash PMI revealed stronger manufacturing output and job creation, although inflation pressures remained high, with firms raising selling prices in response.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is India's wheat export push a good idea?
articles.summary: India has reopened wheat exports amid a record surplus to ease excess supply and support farm prices. By offloading stocks, it aims to boost farmer incomes without stoking inflation, as ample buffer reserves and policy flexibility help keep domestic supply stable.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil prices up amid stalemate over next round of peace talks, continued blockade of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices ticked higher as the US–Iran standoff over the Strait of Hormuz deepened, with naval blockades continuing despite ceasefire claims, raising supply and inflation risks for India.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Caution at MPC as war muddies view
articles.summary: The minutes showed that while members expect inflation to rise further, it would be primarily driven by supply shocks, something that monetary policy has limited control over.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% in March as Iran war sends fuel prices to highest rise since Ukraine invasion
articles.summary: UK inflation rose to 3.3% in March due to surging energy costs from the Iran war. Motor fuel prices jumped 8.7%, while services inflation unexpectedly increased to 4.5%. This inflation outlook may impact interest rates, with economists now expecting a prolonged pause in rate hikes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India likely to grow 7% in FY27 on domestic demand, investments; inflation to remain in range: EAC-PM chairman Dev
articles.summary: The economy’s resilience in the face of headwinds generated by the West Asia war could be demonstrated by the narrowing of India’s trade deficit to $20.67 billion in March, said S. Mahendra Dev, chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to PM.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil prices trade lower as Trump announces ceasefire extension; naval blockade of Iran curbs losses
articles.summary: Global oil prices eased after the US extended its ceasefire with Iran, but a continuing naval blockade and the prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz kept supply concerns—and volatility—firmly in play.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s GDP growth to slow to 6.4% in FY27 amid geopolitical headwinds: UN report
articles.summary: The report, by the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific, projected that India’s GDP growth would pick up moderately to 6.6% in FY28, driven by domestic demand and services.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: From crude to monsoon risks: Indian consumer demand splits under inflation pressure
articles.summary: Lower-income households are cutting back, while affluent consumers turn cautious, with the divide beginning to show up in FMCG and durables demand.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil prices subdued as traders await clarity over ceasefire extension, next round of US-Iran talks
articles.summary: Brent slips below $95 as traders await clarity on US–Iran ceasefire extension and peace talks, even as Strait of Hormuz tensions and naval seizures heighten supply risks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Champagne raises a toast to growth in India, duty cuts to add to the fizz
articles.summary: Under the proposed India-EU free trade agreement, duties on champagne are set to fall from 150% to 20% over seven years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India, South Korea working to double bilateral trade by 2030: Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: Both countries have been tasked by their leadership to raise two-way trade from the current $27 billion to $54 billion by 2030, says commerce minister Piyush Goyal
articles.label: NEUTRAL
